name: Indian Tamarind Chutney
recipeIngredient: 1/4 teaspoon red chile pepper (up to 1/2 teaspoon)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the tamarind and boiling water; let soak for at least 1 hour.
2. Soak the raisins in hot water for 1 hour, then puree them in a blender and set aside.
3. Squeeze the tamarind with fingers to break it up, then push through a sieve, rubbing as much pulp through as possible.
4. Scrape the back of the sieve to collect any remaining pulp.
5. Stir in the pureed raisins and all the remaining ingredients. Blend the mixture well.
6. Cover and let stand at room temperature for several hours.
7. Serve with fried foods and savory pastries. It will keep in the refrigerator for several days.
Variation: Add thin slices of 1 banana and serve as a relish.
